summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for Moltis with full parity to existing channels (Telegram, Discord, etc.), including backend crate, web UI, onboarding, docs, metrics, NIP-44 support, and integration tests.
summary.outcome: Delivered a complete moltis-nostr crate (~2200 LOC, 23 unit tests, 5 integration tests) with all channel checklist items satisfied, including a subsequent round of PR review fixes covering OTP flow correctness, config sharing, and a tokio RwLock panic in e2e tests.

repo: ChannelType enum uses #[non_exhaustive] so new variants don't require touching exhaustive matches elsewhere in the gateway/chat crates
Channel account maps must use std::sync::RwLock, not tokio::sync::RwLock — Telegram and Matrix both follow this pattern; tokio::sync::RwLock::blocking_read() panics inside a tokio runtime
The matrix channel was going through serde #[flatten] extra field, not a named ChannelsConfig field — adding it as a named field is a safe but unintended side-effect

OTP flow: notification receiver must be created BEFORE client.connect() to avoid missing messages; nostr-sdk Event notifications deduplicate so RelayPoolNotification::Message must be used for reliable DM receipt
Biome (not rustfmt) handles JS formatting — CI runs cargo +nightly fmt AND biome check; chained method calls must be split across lines per biome rules
Integration tests use #[ignore] and are run separately with env vars; pattern matches provider integration tests in crates/providers/tests/
channels.offered default list drives the web UI connect buttons — nostr must be added there to appear in the picker
OTP verification must happen BEFORE the access gate in the event pipeline; otherwise the code DM is rejected as unauthorized before it can be checked

code.finding: tokio::sync::RwLock::blocking_read() panics when called from within a tokio runtime; account maps must use std::sync::RwLock throughout
client.notifications() broadcast receiver must be bound before connect() — creating it after misses messages already dispatched
Nested if-let inside match arm triggers clippy::collapsible_match — must be collapsed into match arm guard using && syntax
nostr_sdk::prelude::* imports both nip04 and nip44 decrypt functions creating a name collision; must use fully qualified nostr_sdk::nips::nip04::decrypt path
ChannelsConfig named_fields() and Default impl must both be updated when adding a new named channel field
clippy::len_without_is_empty fires if a struct has len() but no is_empty() — must add is_empty() method
Optional dep feature activation in Cargo requires dep?/feature syntax (weak dependency) to avoid pulling in an optional dep when only the feature flag is enabled without the dep itself

workflow: nostr-sdk v0.44: nip04 and nip44 features are NOT in default — must be explicitly enabled; subscribe() takes single Filter not Vec<Filter>; disconnect() returns () not Result; to_bech32() returns Result<String, Infallible>
std::sync::RwLockReadGuard is !Send — cannot hold it across .await; must extract values into owned locals before any await point

For truly unrelated git histories (squashed main + feature branch), cherry-pick individual commits onto a new branch from origin/main rather than attempting --allow-unrelated-histories merge which produces 70+ conflicts
Greptile review loop: trigger review, poll for completion, fetch comment bodies, fix, re-trigger — repeat until 0 comments

friction: nostr-sdk v0.44 API surface required multiple compilation rounds to discover: NIP-04 not in default features, subscribe() takes Filter not Vec, disconnect() is infallible, to_bech32() returns Result<_, Infallible>
nostr_sdk::prelude::* wildcard import creates silent name collision between nip04::decrypt and nip44::decrypt — compiler error message was misleading (unnecessary qualification) rather than ambiguity

tokio::sync::RwLock panic in e2e tests only surfaced after full e2e run, not during cargo test — root cause (blocking_read inside runtime) required reading Telegram/Matrix source to identify the correct pattern
DM round-trip integration test required three attempts to get working: wrong env var names, notifications receiver created after connect(), Event dedup requiring Message variant instead
Merging main with unrelated histories (squashed single commit vs full branch history) produced 70+ conflicts — required abort and cherry-pick strategy
OTP verification was silently broken (access gate ran before OTP check) — only caught by automated PR review, not unit tests
open_items: NIP-44 outbound preference (config option to prefer NIP-44 over NIP-04 for sending) — currently always sends NIP-04
NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs — future enhancement, requires more complex event handling, less widely supported
NIP-65 relay list advertisement — future, advertise preferred relays
Metrics feature has counters/gauges defined but no dashboards or alerting configured
Contract test suite (moltis_channels::contract) not run against NostrPlugin — requires valid credentials to start so can't use TestPlugin pattern directly
seen.rs LRU persistence to data_dir()/nostr_seen.json with debounced writes — currently in-memory only, state lost on restart
